title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After discussing the possibility of their garage being compromised, Frankie and Bobby decide to meet at the DeWinter city residence on Brook Street. Frankie reveals new information regarding the day Bobby was poisoned: while Barclay-French was in Stavely, Nicolson was not in London for a scheduled conference, and he drove a deep blue Talbot. Bobby notes that Nicolson could also obtain morphine, which aligns with their existing clues.

Frankie uses a phone book to search for the name "Rivington" and identifies Colonel Rivington, a Distinguished Service Cross recipient living on Tite Street, Chelsea, as a likely lead. To investigate, Frankie instructs Bobby to impersonate a new partner at a law firm. She provides him with a business card for "Spragg-Spragg-Jenkinson & Spragg, Esqs." of Bloomsbury Square, belonging to her father's lawyers. To complete the disguise, Bobby borrows a morning suit and striped trousers from the Marquess of Cheldon’s wardrobe.

Bobby travels to Tite Street and uses the fake business card to gain entry to the Rivington home. He meets Mrs. Rivington, who is easily swayed by his professional appearance and a fabricated claim that his office called ahead. Bobby pretends to represent their client, Mr. Allen Castles. Mrs. Rivington confirms that Castles is staying at the Savoy Hotel and mentions that the last time they saw him was at Stafholt, after which they visited the Bassington-Frenches. She also notes that Castles likely came to England because of a millionaire friend who died by suicide after being diagnosed with cancer.
